_notes: Natural-language descriptions for each Ghost B predicate. Used as zero-shot label signals AND as canonical ontology reference. Edit deliberately — these shape model behavior.
part_of: Subject is a component, module, section, or constituent of Object. Object contains, comprises, or is composed of Subject.
uses: Subject employs, leverages, utilizes, applies, or operates via Object to perform its function.
implements: Subject realizes, conforms to, or provides an interface for Object. Object is an abstract specification, protocol, or contract.
depends_on: Subject requires Object to function. Subject is built on top of Object or treats Object as a prerequisite.
supports: Subject enables, facilitates, allows, or helps Object happen. Object would not be possible (or would be harder) without Subject.
produces: Subject generates, outputs, yields, or emits Object as a result of its operation.
stores: Subject persists, caches, or holds Object as data. Object is the artifact being stored; Subject is the storage system.
detects: Subject identifies, recognizes, classifies, or finds instances of Object during operation.
member_of: Subject is a constituent member, participant, or affiliate of group Object (Organization or Concept).
instance_of: Subject is a concrete example or specific case of category Object. Object is an abstract concept; Subject is a realization.
example_of: Subject is given as a representative illustration of Object. Often introduced by 'such as', 'for example', 'e.g.'.
created_by: Subject was authored, written, designed, developed, or made by Object (Person or Organization).
works_for: Subject (Person) is employed by, contracted to, or a staff member of Object (Organization).
owns: Subject possesses, controls, or has ownership of Object.
affiliated_with: Subject is partnered with, allied to, or associated with Object without full membership or employment.
located_in: Subject is physically situated, based, found, or stored at Object (Location).
during: Subject occurs concurrently with, while, or throughout Object. Both are temporal Events or processes.
preceded_by: Subject occurs after Object in time. Object happens first; Subject follows. Inverse of 'precedes'.
overlaps: Subject and Object share a temporal interval; they coincide in part of their durations.
causes: Subject leads to, results in, triggers, or brings about Object. Object is a consequence of Subject.
contradicts: Subject conflicts with, opposes, refutes, or is inconsistent with Object. Both cannot simultaneously hold.
overrides: Subject supersedes, replaces, takes precedence over, or preempts Object.
excepts: Subject applies except in the case of Object. Object is an exclusion or carve-out from Subject.
defines: Subject specifies, formalizes, or gives the meaning of Object. Object is a term, concept, or specification.
represents: Subject stands for, denotes, or symbolizes Object. Subject is a sign or proxy for Object.
maps_to: Subject corresponds to, is equivalent to, or translates into Object across two systems or vocabularies.
synonym_of: Subject and Object refer to the same thing; one is another name for the other.
references: Subject cites, mentions, or points to Object (a Document, Concept, or Standard) as a source or related work.
derived_from: Subject is based on, adapted from, extends, or inherits from Object.
no_relation: Subject and Object co-occur in the same context but no specific predicate from this ontology applies. Use this when the pair is real but the relation is absent, ambiguous, or outside the vocabulary.
